name: Embalse de la Breña - circular from Almodóvar del Río
name_original: Embalse de la Breña – circular desde Almodóvar del Río
region_intro: Hiking around Almodóvar Del Río offers diverse landscapes within the Guadalquivir Valley, including the Sierra de Hornachuelos Natural Park. The region features dense forests, dramatic cliffs, and riverbank ecosystems along the Guadalquivir River. Key natural elements include the expansive La Breña Reservoir and the elevated Almodóvar Castle, providing panoramic views. Best hiking trails around Almodóvar Del Río The most popular hiking route is Embalse de la Breña - circular from Almodóvar del Río, a 7.2 miles (11.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 11 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the second-largest reservoir in Andalusia. Another top favourite among local hikers is View of Almodóvar del Río – Almodóvar del Río Castle loop from Almodóvar del Río, a moderate 5.9 miles (9.4 km) path. This trail provides views of the historic Almodóvar Castle and the surrounding river plain. Local hikers also love the Castle of Almodóvar del Río – View of La Breña Reservoir loop from Almodóvar del Río, a 9.1 miles (14.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 58 minutes. Hiking around Almodóvar Del Río is defined by the Sierra de Hornachuelos Natural Park, the La Breña Reservoir, and the Guadalquivir River. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Almodóvar Del Río are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 200 reviews. Nearly 1,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Almodóvar Del Río's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Almodóvar Del Río?
A: Almodóvar Del Río offers a wide variety of hiking opportunities, with over 60 routes available. These range from easy strolls to more challenging treks, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Almodóvar Del Río?
A: Yes, Almodóvar Del Río has 17 easy hiking routes that are perfect for beginners or families. These trails often explore the pleasant riverbank areas along the Guadalquivir or offer gentle paths around the La Breña Reservoir.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Almodóvar Del Río?
A: Hiking in Almodóvar Del Río offers diverse landscapes. You'll encounter dense forests of cork oaks and holm oaks, dramatic cliffs, and canyons within the Sierra de Hornachuelos Natural Park. Many routes also feature stunning views of the La Breña Reservoir and tranquil riverine forests along the Guadalquivir River.

Q: Are there any circular walks in Almodóvar Del Río?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Almodóvar Del Río are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995853178/embalse-de-la-brena-circular-from-almodovar-del-rio" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de la Breña - circular from Almodóvar del Río&lt;/a&gt;, which offers beautiful reservoir views.

Q: What are some notable attractions or landmarks I can see along the hiking trails?
A: Many trails offer views of the majestic Almodóvar Castle, perched on a hill overlooking the town. You can also explore natural features like the La Breña Reservoir. For specific points of interest, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/624215" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baños de Popea Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2928593" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Mine and Pools&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of the Almodóvar Castle?
A: Absolutely. The Almodóvar Castle is a prominent landmark. A great route to experience its grandeur is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24522994" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Almodóvar del Río – Almodóvar del Río Castle loop from Almodóvar del Río&lt;/a&gt;, which provides excellent views of the historic castle and the surrounding river plain.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Almodóvar Del Río?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 250 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning views of the La Breña Reservoir, and the historical context provided by the Almodóvar Castle.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by the La Breña Reservoir?
A: Yes, the La Breña Reservoir is a central feature of the region. Many trails offer views of its expansive waters. A good option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122132" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Almodóvar del Río – View of La Breña Reservoir loop from Almodóvar del Río&lt;/a&gt;, which combines castle views with reservoir scenery.

Q: What is the typical duration for a moderate hike in this region?
A: Moderate hikes in Almodóvar Del Río can vary in length and duration. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1248122664/dehesilla-de-guadalcazar-and-via-verde-de-la-campina-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesilla de Guadalcázar and Vía Verde de la Campiña Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 14.7 km route that typically takes around 3 hours and 52 minutes to complete.

Q: Can I find trails that explore the Sierra de Hornachuelos Natural Park?
A: Yes, a significant portion of Almodóvar Del Río falls within the Sierra de Hornachuelos Natural Park. Many trails will take you through its characteristic dense forests, dramatic cliffs, and canyons, offering a rich environment for exploration and wildlife spotting.

Q: Are there any specific trails known for their natural beauty or unique features?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. Trails around the La Breña Reservoir are particularly noted for their wonderful water-bathed landscapes. The Sierra de Hornachuelos Natural Park offers routes through ancient cork oak and holm oak forests, providing a unique natural experience.
tips: The castle complex, whose origins date back to the 8th century, is remarkably well preserved and has been restored with great attention to detail.

The old La Breña dam built between 1931 and 1935 is currently submerged since its reservoir capacity was insufficient to store the abundant contributions from the Guadiato basin, which is why the reservoir was expanded by building La Breña. II. The La Breña I dam was located 120 m upstream of La Breña II.
Mooring on the Guadalquivir river whose origin dates back to Roman times, showing how old this river was navigable.
